activities_projects.title: Comprehensive Local Economy Survey
activities_projects.objective: To analyze the distribution of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector activities in the local area.
steps: Design a survey covering occupation, income type, and sector classification.
Collect data from at least 20 households.
Classify responses sector-wise and prepare charts with analysis.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand real-world sector distribution and develop data analysis skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess survey design, data accuracy, sector classification, and quality of analysis.

activities_projects.title: GDP vs Employment Mismatch Research
activities_projects.objective: To examine the gap between GDP contribution and employment share among sectors.
activities_projects.materials: Textbook data, internet resources, graph paper
steps: Collect recent GDP contribution and employment data of each sector.
Create comparative graphs.
Analyze reasons for mismatch and suggest policy improvements.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically evaluate structural imbalances in the Indian economy.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate data representation, analytical depth, and logical suggestions.

activities_projects.title: Organized vs Unorganized Sector Case Study
activities_projects.objective: To compare working conditions in organized and unorganized sectors.
steps: Interview one worker from organized and one from unorganized sector.
Compare wages, job security, benefits, and working hours.
Prepare a detailed comparative report.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand employment conditions and labor rights differences.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess quality of interviews, comparison clarity, and conclusion strength.

activities_projects.title: Service Sector Growth Analysis
activities_projects.objective: To research the rapid growth of the tertiary sector in India.
steps: Collect data on service sector growth over the last 10 years.
Identify major drivers like IT, banking, and telecom.
Prepare a presentation explaining its impact on GDP and employment.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand reasons behind tertiary sector expansion.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check research accuracy, clarity of explanation, and presentation skills.

activities_projects.title: Interdependence Documentary Project
activities_projects.objective: To demonstrate how different sectors depend on each other.
steps: Record examples of activities from all three sectors in your locality.
Explain how each sector supports the others.
Create a 5-minute documentary with narration.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess creativity, clarity, and logical explanation.

activities_projects.title: Public vs Private Sector Company Analysis
activities_projects.objective: To compare objectives and performance of public and private companies.
steps: Select one public sector and one private sector company.
Compare their objectives, services, and contributions.
Prepare a comparative research report.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand differences in ownership and goals.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate research depth and comparison accuracy.

activities_projects.title: Technology Impact Study Across Sectors
activities_projects.objective: To analyze how technology has transformed each sector.
steps: Identify technological changes in agriculture, industry, and services.
Analyze productivity improvements due to technology.
Present findings with real-life examples.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand modernization and digital transformation in sectors.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess relevance of examples and analytical explanation.

activities_projects.title: Income Inequality Across Sectors Research
activities_projects.objective: To examine income variations between sectors.
steps: Collect average income data from at least three occupations (one per sector).
Compare annual incomes using charts.
Analyze reasons for income differences.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand wage disparities and economic inequality.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check calculation accuracy and depth of reasoning.

activities_projects.title: Rural vs Urban Sector Comparison
activities_projects.objective: To compare sector dominance in rural and urban areas.
steps: Collect employment data from one rural and one urban locality.
Prepare tables comparing sector-wise distribution.
Analyze key differences and suggest improvements.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate comparison quality, data clarity, and logical suggestions.

activities_projects.title: Future of Indian Economy 2040 Blueprint
activities_projects.objective: To design a long-term economic plan focusing on balanced sector growth.
steps: Identify present challenges in each sector.
Propose innovative solutions for balanced development.
Present a structured development blueprint with justification.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply research, creativity, and strategic thinking to economic planning.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess creativity, feasibility, research support, and clarity of presentation.
